name: Trail Mix Tin Can Quick Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. For fruit: Use dried cranberries, cherries, blueberries, currants, or raisins.
2. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
3. Grease and flour two 1-pound coffee cans or 2 9x5-inch loaf pans and line bottoms with wax paper. Grease and flour the wax paper.
4. Place the fruit bits or raisins in a large bowl, cover with hot water, and stir in baking soda. Cover with plastic wrap and leave overnight to plump. Do not drain the liquid; it will be used in the batter.
5. In a separate bowl, mix together the fruit bits (or raisins), soaking water, sugar, oil, salt, flour, nuts, cinnamon (if using), and almond extract (if using). Stir until well blended. If the batter is too thin to spoon, add a small amount of additional flour.
6. Spoon the batter into the prepared cans or loaf pans, filling them 1/2 to 2/3 full. Use a spatula to press the batter down into the bottom of each can or pan.
7. Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour, or until deeply browned and cooked through.
8. Remove the bread from the oven and let it sit in the containers for 10 minutes before turning off the heat source. Then, turn off the heat source and slide the bread out of the cans onto wire racks to cool completely.
9. Once cooled, return the loaves to the cans and cover with plastic lids or tightly wrap in plastic. The bread will keep for 3 or 4 months when stored in the refrigerator.
